content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom labels. Looking at atom C:52 (carbon with index 52), I can see it's connected to:

- C:51 (carbon, index 51) - connected by a yellow bond
- N:53 (nitrogen, index 53) - connected by a blue bond
- C:50 (carbon, index 50) - connected by a yellow bond

From the image, I can confirm that atom 52 has exactly three covalent bonds to these three atoms. There are no other atoms directly bonded to C:52.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 199, as indicated by the file name "geomconf_199.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom 52 (C) are:
- Atom index 50, element C
- Atom index 51, element C  
- Atom index 53, element N

And the geomconf_index is 199.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 52, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
